reason: The sole finding is a silent correction of an unambiguous source typo ("ファーマット" → "フォーマット"). The corrected text is semantically identical and more accurate. An AI agent reading either form would reach the same understanding, so there is zero adverse user impact.

findings_assessment.location: s5 — コード値の表示 table, WebView_CodeTag row
findings_assessment.justification: The source text 'ファーマット' is a clear OCR/transcription typo for 'フォーマット' (format). The knowledge file's correction does not introduce any false information — the semantic content is identical. No AI agent consuming this knowledge would be misled or behave differently. Phase E retained the corrected form because reverting to the typo would have degraded fidelity without any benefit, and the fabrication classification itself is borderline: correcting an obvious single-character kana transposition is not the same as inventing content.
